sourceMovie.title: Escape from Alcatraz
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Escape from Alcatraz (1979) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Room
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Escape from Alcatraz clicked for you, Room (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shot Caller
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Escape from Alcatraz clicked for you, Shot Caller (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Escape from Alcatraz?
faq.answer: Try Room, Shot Caller, The Informer—each shares DNA with Escape from Alcatraz while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Escape from Alcatraz?
faq.answer: Escape from Alcatraz (1979) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Escape from Alcatraz worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
